mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate transmits through discount-rate compression and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already near the lower bound and inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if lower FEDFUNDS levels do not precede higher 20d SPX returns, or if the sign reverses in easing periods and the model shows negative average forward returns when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which depresses earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average return after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS relative to its recent level signals stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports near-term equity returns as analysts raise sales expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising RSAFS do not correspond to above-zero 20d SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year rank correlation between RSAFS change and forward returns stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO together with rising HOUST marks broad cyclical production recovery, which improves cyclicals and earnings breadth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in INDPRO and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A very low and stable FEDFUNDS rate reduces the equity risk premium through cheaper financing and a stronger present value of future cash flows, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 1.0% is not followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than chance, or if the mean 5d return after the trigger is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS combined with a falling UNRATE indicates genuine labor-market tightening and stronger disposable income, which raises earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE combination does not outperform each standalone series in predicting 20d SPX returns, or if positive payroll changes with rising unemployment are followed by negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows while RSAFS weakens, the economy is entering a demand deterioration phase that lowers earnings revisions and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS is not associated with below-zero 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term becomes positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in INDPRO after a prior expansion signals an industrial slowdown that feeds into weaker revenue momentum and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by at least 0.5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is not negative.
